title: Quiz: Cloud Quantum Computing Cost Optimization

questions.question: What is IBM Quantum's primary pricing model?
questions.explanation: IBM Quantum charges based on quantum execution time at approximately $1.60 per second. Queue waiting time is free; you only pay for actual execution time on the quantum processor.

questions.question: On Amazon Braket, what components make up the total job cost?
questions.explanation: Amazon Braket uses a hybrid model with a per-shot cost (varies by device) plus a per-task base cost ($0.30 for most QPUs). Total cost = (shots x shot_price) + task_price.

questions.question: To achieve 1% precision in probability estimates at 95% confidence, approximately how many shots are needed?
questions.explanation: Using the formula n = z^2 * p * (1-p) / precision^2, with z=1.96, p=0.5, and precision=0.01, we get approximately 9,604 shots, or about 10,000 shots.

questions.question: Which circuit modification would most reduce costs on a per-gate pricing model?

options.text: Reducing two-qubit gate count through optimization

questions.explanation: On per-gate pricing (like IonQ on Azure), two-qubit gates typically cost 10x more than single-qubit gates. Reducing two-qubit gate count through circuit optimization provides the biggest cost savings.

questions.question: What is the purpose of adaptive shot allocation across multiple circuits?

options.text: To make all circuits use the same number of shots
To allocate more shots to higher-priority circuits within a fixed budget
To reduce the total number of circuits

questions.explanation: Adaptive shot allocation distributes a fixed budget across multiple circuits based on their importance or weight. More critical circuits receive more shots for better precision, while less critical ones use fewer shots.

questions.question: Why is tracking daily spending rate useful for budget management?

options.text: To project end-of-month costs and detect potential overruns early

questions.explanation: Tracking daily spending rate allows you to project whether you'll exceed your monthly budget before it happens. This enables proactive cost control measures rather than reactive surprises.

questions.question: Queue waiting time is typically charged on most cloud quantum platforms.
questions.explanation: False. Queue waiting time is generally free across major providers. You only pay for actual execution time (IBM) or per-shot/task (Braket). This is important because queue times can be hours while execution is seconds.

questions.question: Which provider would be most cost-effective for running 100 shots of a simple circuit?
questions.explanation: If free tier minutes are available, IBM Quantum would be free. Otherwise, Rigetti ($0.335 total) beats IonQ ($3.30 total) for 100 shots due to the much lower per-shot cost.
